Reconstructing a single coherent thesis is structurally necessary regardless of which framing is correct.","R1.2":"The logical arc is broken: the 8.2pp accuracy gain is introduced as the load-bearing result, then retroactively repositioned as a side-effect of a calibration analysis that appears later. Intermediate claims (critic disagreement resolution, SCCE construction) are not consistently linked to whichever framing is ultimately primary, leaving premise-to-conclusion coherence unresolved.","R1.5":"The narrative order—accuracy claim, then calibration findings, then contribution reframe—buries the stated thesis at the end of the section and would require reorganization to repair regardless of whether the conceptual framing or prose quality is the primary target.","R1.6":"'Help me with this section' is the canonical academic writing-assistance phrasing and makes prose polish an active expected responsibility until scope is narrowed."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary goal to resolve the structural and conceptual conflict—specifically that the section opens by naming an accuracy protocol as the central contribution and then ends by reframing the central claim as a calibration diagnosis, leaving the thesis contradictory—or to improve sentence-level prose quality while preserving the current structure and framing as-is?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section contains a self-contradicting contribution claim: paragraph 1 frames the central contribution as a multi-agent accuracy protocol, while paragraph 3 reframes the central claim as a calibration-failure diagnosis, demoting the accuracy gain to a corollary. Reconstructing a single coherent thesis is structurally necessary regardless of which framing is correct.
rationale.R1.2: The logical arc is broken: the 8.2pp accuracy gain is introduced as the load-bearing result, then retroactively repositioned as a side-effect of a calibration analysis that appears later. Intermediate claims (critic disagreement resolution, SCCE construction) are not consistently linked to whichever framing is ultimately primary, leaving premise-to-conclusion coherence unresolved.
rationale.R1.5: The narrative order—accuracy claim, then calibration findings, then contribution reframe—buries the stated thesis at the end of the section and would require reorganization to repair regardless of whether the conceptual framing or prose quality is the primary target.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is the canonical academic writing-assistance phrasing and makes prose polish an active expected responsibility until scope is narrowed.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal to resolve the structural and conceptual conflict—specifically that the section opens by naming an accuracy protocol as the central contribution and then ends by reframing the central claim as a calibration diagnosis, leaving the thesis contradictory—or to improve sentence-level prose quality while preserving the current structure and framing as-is?
